title: glechoma campestris
intro: Glechoma campestris, also known as Creeping Sage or Creeping Thyme, is a perennial herb native to Eastern Europe and Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Glechoma campestris include soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Glechoma campestris can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used as a natural remedy in traditional medicine.
